items.q: Is the independent research project assessed as a written report?
items.correct: No - it is presented and discussed in the speaking examination, where you summarise findings and answer questions on them.
items.misconception: You hand in a long written dossier that is marked separately.
items.random_wrong: It is assessed through a multiple-choice test.
items.why: Knowing the assessment format shapes preparation: the skill tested is sustaining spoken analysis and discussion of your research, not extended writing.

items.q: Is one good source enough for the research project?
items.correct: No - you should compare several varied sources, which lets you contrast viewpoints and judge reliability.
items.misconception: A single detailed article gives you everything you need.
items.random_wrong: Only printed books may be used as sources.
items.why: Examiners reward evaluation and comparison of evidence; a single source cannot show the balanced, critical research the project demands.

items.correct: To carry out or to achieve, as in 'realizar una encuesta' - to carry out a survey.
items.misconception: It means 'to realise' something in your mind.
items.random_wrong: It means 'to make something real estate'.
items.why: 'Realizar' is a high-frequency false friend in research contexts; 'to realise' (become aware) is 'darse cuenta de', a contrast worth mastering for accurate analysis.

items.q: An independent research project for Spanish must focus on what?
items.correct: An aspect of the society or culture of the Spanish-speaking world
items.why: Correct answer: An aspect of the society or culture of the Spanish-speaking world. “Any topic from any country” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What does 'los datos demuestran que...' mean?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The data show that.... “The dates are wrong because...” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: To partially concede a point in a debate, you could say what?
items.correct: Hasta cierto punto, estoy de acuerdo
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Hasta cierto punto, estoy de acuerdo. “No tengo ni idea” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which preparation best supports the discussion of your research?
items.correct: Deep, flexible knowledge of your findings and sources
items.misconception: A memorised script recited word for word
items.random_wrong: Reading the sources for the first time that morning
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Deep, flexible knowledge of your findings and sources. “A memorised script recited word for word” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
